criteria.title: Anonymous registration, posting, and unlinkability
criteria.criterion: A member can register in a forum instance with a stake and subsequently publish posts with valid anonymous proofs of membership. Posts from the same member are unlinkable to any observer, including moderators, while below the slash threshold.

criteria.title: Slash retroactive linkability only for slashed member
criteria.criterion: Upon slash, the reconstructed secret enables retroactive linkability of the slashed member's prior posts. No other member's anonymity is affected. This property is documented in docs/protocol.md.

criteria.title: N-of-M off-chain moderation certificates
criteria.criterion: N-of-M moderators can jointly produce a valid moderation certificate off-chain using the Logos stack. Fewer than N moderators cannot produce a certificate.

criteria.title: K certificates trigger slash and registry revocation
criteria.criterion: When K moderation certificates are accumulated for the same member, any moderator or party can submit a single slash transaction to the membership registry; the registry verifies evidence and revokes membership.

criteria.title: Revocation list rejects future posts
criteria.criterion: A slashed commitment is added to the revocation list. Subsequent posts with a proof tied to that commitment are rejected.

criteria.title: Independent forum K and N-of-M parameters
criteria.criterion: The protocol is parameterisable: forum instances can independently set K and N-of-M.

proofs.name: Forum A and Forum B flow

criteria.title: Forum-agnostic moderation SDK APIs
criteria.criterion: A standalone forum-agnostic moderation library exposes documented APIs for registration, encrypted proof generation and verification, certificate construction, and slash submission; it uses abstract content identifiers and Logos off-chain abstractions.

criteria.title: Basecamp app full moderation lifecycle without CLI
criteria.criterion: A working Logos Basecamp app built on the library allows non-technical users to create a forum, register, post, moderate, view history, and revoke without CLI interaction.

criteria.title: LEZ testnet demo with two independent forums
criteria.criterion: End-to-end demonstration on LEZ testnet includes at least two independent forum instances using different K and N-of-M parameters.

criteria.title: Importable documented moderation SDK
criteria.criterion: Provide the moderation library as an importable module/SDK with documented API for Logos applications.

criteria.title: SPEL IDL for membership registry
criteria.criterion: Provide an IDL for the membership registry LEZ program using the SPEL framework.

criteria.title: Retryable proof generation failures preserve nullifier
criteria.criterion: Proof generation failures surface a clear error to the member and allow retry without consuming the member's nullifier.

criteria.title: Partial certificates cannot reach on-chain slash
criteria.criterion: A partial moderation certificate with fewer than N moderators cannot be submitted on-chain; the library enforces threshold client-side.

criteria.title: Logos stack failures queue and retry actions
criteria.criterion: Transient Logos stack failures are handled gracefully: pending posts and moderation actions are queued and retried rather than silently dropped.

criteria.title: ZK proof generation under ten seconds
criteria.criterion: ZK membership proof generation takes less than 10 seconds on a standard laptop.

criteria.title: Document CU costs for register and slash
criteria.criterion: Document the compute unit cost of each on-chain operation, including registration and slash submission, on LEZ devnet/testnet.

criteria.title: Registry deployed and tested on LEZ devnet/testnet
criteria.criterion: The membership registry program is deployed and tested on LEZ devnet/testnet.

criteria.title: LEZ sequencer local integration diagnostics
criteria.criterion: End-to-end integration tests run against a LEZ sequencer in standalone mode through the reproducible local submission gate; GitHub Actions is not used as acceptance evidence because hosted jobs are blocked.

criteria.title: Local submission gate green while GitHub Actions is blocked
criteria.criterion: The default branch must have reproducible green local acceptance evidence. GitHub Actions is unavailable for this repo because account billing/spending limits block job startup, so submission evidence uses the local integration gate instead.

proofs.name: default branch CI workflow kept for evaluators who can run Actions

criteria.title: README E2E usage and program addresses
criteria.criterion: README documents deployment steps, program addresses, and steps for forum creation, registration, posting, moderation, and slash through Basecamp.

criteria.title: Real local sequencer demo with RISC0_DEV_MODE=0
criteria.criterion: A reproducible end-to-end demo script works against a real local sequencer with RISC0_DEV_MODE=0.

proofs.name: local sequencer deploy plus RISC0_DEV_MODE=0 evidence

criteria.title: Narrated recorded video demo
criteria.criterion: Submission includes narrated video showing architecture, decisions, full lifecycle, terminal output, proof generation, and RISC0_DEV_MODE=0.
